action.reasoning: Observation: predict.py crashed on NameError (torch not imported); heuristic still weak (~48 safe steps avg, 0 success, ~4.6m avg dist) despite tuning. Test ran full 750-step episodes but policy loses track of moving humanoid. Analysis: missing import is root cause of eval failure; P-controller insufficient for dynamic target (human oracle moves randomly); literature shows map-based ShortestPathFollower heuristic reaches ~0.5 follow-rate — but integrating it requires discrete-to-continuous mapping and goal projection for desired 1.5m standoff. For now, fix import, remove artificial 500-step cap, retune gains (higher angular, velocity damping, stronger forward bias on detector), add explicit config inspection for facing threshold. Decision: update test_full_episode.py + predict.py with fixes + new heuristic v3 (angular=-3.5*angle, linear=clip(1.0*error,-0.7,1.0), detector boost, backup logic), run test to measure improvement/time, then submit_eval on fixed predict.py (still dummy ckpt).
